description: Fix two concrete issues found during browser extension audit: stale Chrome notifications that linger after confirmation resolution, and dead production code (exported but never imported).

userStories.title: Dismiss Chrome notifications when confirmations are resolved or expire
userStories.description: As a user, I want confirmation notifications to be dismissed automatically when I respond to or when they expire, so that I don't see stale 'Approval Required' notifications in my OS notification center.
acceptanceCriteria: When sp:confirmationResponse is handled in background-message-handlers.ts, chrome.notifications.clear() is called for the corresponding notification ID (opentabs-confirm-${id})
When sp:confirmationTimeout is handled in background-message-handlers.ts, chrome.notifications.clear() is called for the corresponding notification ID
When the background-side timeout fires in confirmation-badge.ts (line 49-53), chrome.notifications.clear() is called for the corresponding notification ID
When clearAllConfirmationBadges() is called (on disconnect), all opentabs-confirm-* notifications are cleared
Existing unit tests for confirmation-badge continue to pass
userStories.notes:
The bug is in platform/browser-extension/src/confirmation-badge.ts and platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts.

In confirmation-badge.ts:
- Line 56-65: chrome.notifications.create() creates notifications with ID `opentabs-confirm-${id}` and requireInteraction: true
- Line 104-116: chrome.notifications.onClicked handler calls chrome.notifications.clear() — this is the ONLY place notifications are dismissed
- Line 49-53: background timeout fires clearConfirmationBadge() but does NOT clear the notification
- Line 89-96: clearAllConfirmationBadges() clears all timeouts and badge but does NOT clear notifications

In background-message-handlers.ts:
- Lines 170-179: handleSpConfirmationResponse calls clearConfirmationBackgroundTimeout and clearConfirmationBadge but does NOT clear the notification
- Lines 182-188: handleSpConfirmationTimeout calls clearConfirmationBackgroundTimeout and clearConfirmationBadge but does NOT clear the notification

Fix approach:
1. In confirmation-badge.ts, add chrome.notifications.clear(`opentabs-confirm-${id}`).catch(() => {}) in the background timeout handler (around line 51)
2. Add a new exported function clearConfirmationNotification(id: string) that calls chrome.notifications.clear(`opentabs-confirm-${id}`).catch(() => {})
3. In clearAllConfirmationBadges(), also clear all tracked notification IDs. You'll need to track active confirmation IDs (the confirmationTimeouts map keys are available, but also need to track IDs that had timeoutMs=0). Consider tracking notification IDs in a separate Set, or iterating confirmationTimeouts keys before clearing.
4. In background-message-handlers.ts, call the new function in handleSpConfirmationResponse and handleSpConfirmationTimeout

Alternatively, you could fold the notification clearing into clearConfirmationBadge() and clearConfirmationBackgroundTimeout() directly, passing the confirmation ID. The simplest approach: make clearConfirmationBadge accept an optional id parameter and clear the notification when provided.

userStories.title: Remove dead teardownAdapterInTab export
userStories.description: As a developer, I want dead code removed from the codebase so that the exported API surface accurately reflects what is used in production.
acceptanceCriteria: teardownAdapterInTab is no longer exported from iife-injection.ts (it can remain as a private function if still called internally, or be deleted entirely if unused)
All test files that reference teardownAdapterInTab are updated (remove mocks, imports, and test cases for this function)
userStories.notes:
Dead code location: platform/browser-extension/src/iife-injection.ts

- Line 259-282: teardownAdapterInTab is defined as a standalone function
- Line 547: exported in the export block
- The function IS NOT imported by any production code. Teardown is handled inline by cleanupAdaptersInMatchingTabs (lines 453-508) which has its own inline teardown logic in the executeScript func.

Test references to update:
- platform/browser-extension/src/iife-injection.test.ts line 43: imported in test
- platform/browser-extension/src/iife-injection.test.ts lines 203-227: test suite 'teardownAdapterInTab' — delete this entire describe block
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.test.ts line 198: mocked as vi.fn(() => Promise.resolve()) — remove from mock

The function itself can be deleted entirely since no production code calls it. cleanupAdaptersInMatchingTabs (line 453) does its own teardown inline via chrome.scripting.executeScript.
